message: Root user in the account was last accessed 0 days ago.
status_detail: Root user in the account was last accessed 0 days ago.

finding_info.desc: Avoid the use of the root account
finding_info.title: Avoid the use of the root accounts

remediation.desc: Follow the remediation instructions of the Ensure IAM policies are attached only to groups or roles recommendation.

risk_details: The root account has unrestricted access to all resources in the AWS account. It is highly recommended that the use of this account be avoided.

message: AWS policy AmazonEC2ContainerRegistryPowerUser is attached but does not allow '*:*' administrative privileges.
status_detail: AWS policy AmazonEC2ContainerRegistryPowerUser is attached but does not allow '*:*' administrative privileges.

finding_info.desc: Ensure IAM AWS-Managed policies that allow full "*:*" administrative privileges are not attached
finding_info.title: Ensure IAM AWS-Managed policies that allow full "*:*" administrative privileges are not attached

remediation.desc: It is more secure to start with a minimum set of permissions and grant additional permissions as necessary, rather than starting with permissions that are too lenient and then trying to tighten them later. List policies an analyze if permissions are the least possible to conduct business activities.

risk_details: IAM policies are the means by which privileges are granted to users, groups, or roles. It is recommended and considered a standard security advice to grant least privilege—that is, granting only the permissions required to perform a task. Determine what users need to do and then craft policies for them that let the users perform only those tasks instead of allowing full administrative privileges. Providing full administrative privileges instead of restricting to the minimum set of permissions that the user is required to do exposes the resources to potentially unwanted actions.

message: No SAML Providers found.
status_detail: No SAML Providers found.

finding_info.desc: Check if there are SAML Providers then STS can be used
finding_info.title: Check if there are SAML Providers then STS can be used

remediation.desc: Enable SAML provider and use temporary credentials. You can use temporary security credentials to make programmatic requests for AWS resources using the AWS CLI or AWS API (using the AWS SDKs ). The temporary credentials provide the same permissions that you have with use long-term security credentials such as IAM user credentials. In case of not having SAML provider capabilities prevent usage of long-lived credentials.

risk_details: Without SAML provider users with AWS CLI or AWS API access can use IAM static credentials. SAML helps users to assume role by default each time they authenticate.

message: Inline policy CspmMonitorAws attached to role CspmMonitorAws does not allow privilege escalation.
status_detail: Inline policy CspmMonitorAws attached to role CspmMonitorAws does not allow privilege escalation.

finding_info.desc: Ensure no Inline IAM policies allow actions that may lead into Privilege Escalation
finding_info.title: Ensure no IAM Inline policies allow actions that may lead into Privilege Escalation

remediation.desc: Grant usage permission on a per-resource basis and applying least privilege principle.

risk_details: Users with some IAM permissions are allowed to elevate their privileges up to administrator rights.

message: Root account does not have access keys.
status_detail: Root account does not have access keys.

finding_info.desc: Ensure no root account access key exists
finding_info.title: Ensure no root account access key exists

remediation.desc: Use the credential report to check the user and ensure the access_key_1_active and access_key_2_active fields are set to FALSE. If using AWS Organizations, consider enabling Centralized Root Management and removing individual root credentials.

risk_details: The root account is the most privileged user in an AWS account. AWS Access Keys provide programmatic access to a given AWS account. It is recommended that all access keys associated with the root account be removed. Removing access keys associated with the root account limits vectors by which the account can be compromised. Removing the root access keys encourages the creation and use of role based accounts that are least privileged.

message: Password expiration is not set.
status_detail: Password expiration is not set.

finding_info.desc: Ensure IAM password policy expires passwords within 90 days or less
finding_info.title: Ensure IAM password policy expires passwords within 90 days or less

remediation.desc: Ensure Password expiration period (in days): is set to 90 or less.

risk_details: Password policies are used to enforce password complexity requirements. IAM password policies can be used to ensure password are comprised of different character sets. It is recommended that the password policy require at least one uppercase letter.

message: IAM password policy does not require at least one lowercase letter.
status_detail: IAM password policy does not require at least one lowercase letter.

finding_info.desc: Ensure IAM password policy requires at least one uppercase letter
finding_info.title: Ensure IAM password policy require at least one lowercase letter

remediation.desc: Ensure "Requires at least one lowercase letter" is checked under "Password Policy".

risk_details: Password policies are used to enforce password complexity requirements. IAM password policies can be used to ensure password are comprised of different character sets. It is recommended that the password policy require at least one lowercase letter.

message: IAM password policy does not require minimum length of 14 characters.

finding_info.desc: Ensure IAM password policy requires minimum length of 14 or greater
finding_info.title: Ensure IAM password policy requires minimum length of 14 or greater

remediation.desc: Ensure "Minimum password length" is checked under "Password Policy".

risk_details: Password policies are used to enforce password complexity requirements. IAM password policies can be used to ensure password are comprised of different character sets. It is recommended that the password policy require minimum length of 14 or greater.

message: IAM password policy does not require at least one number.

finding_info.title: Ensure IAM password policy require at least one number

remediation.desc: Ensure "Require at least one number" is checked under "Password Policy".

risk_details: Password policies are used to enforce password complexity requirements. IAM password policies can be used to ensure password are comprised of different character sets. It is recommended that the password policy require at least one number.

message: IAM password policy reuse prevention is less than 24 or not set.

finding_info.title: Ensure IAM password policy prevents password reuse: 24 or greater

remediation.desc: Ensure "Number of passwords to remember" is set to 24.

risk_details: Password policies are used to enforce password complexity requirements. IAM password policies can be used to ensure password are comprised of different character sets. It is recommended that the password policy prevents at least password reuse of 24 or greater.

message: User prowler-scan-bot has the policy AdministratorAccess attached.
status_detail: User prowler-scan-bot has the policy AdministratorAccess attached.

finding_info.desc: Ensure IAM policies are attached only to groups or roles
finding_info.title: Ensure IAM policies are attached only to groups or roles

remediation.desc: Remove any policy attached directly to the user. Use groups or roles instead.

risk_details: By default IAM users, groups, and roles have no access to AWS resources. IAM policies are the means by which privileges are granted to users, groups, or roles. It is recommended that IAM policies be applied directly to groups and roles but not users. Assigning privileges at the group or role level reduces the complexity of access management as the number of users grow. Reducing access management complexity may in-turn reduce opportunity for a principal to inadvertently receive or retain excessive privileges.

message: User prowler-scan-bot has the policy SecurityAudit attached.
status_detail: User prowler-scan-bot has the policy SecurityAudit attached.

finding_info.desc: Ensure IAM policies are attached only to groups or roles
finding_info.title: Ensure IAM policies are attached only to groups or roles

remediation.desc: Remove any policy attached directly to the user. Use groups or roles instead.

risk_details: By default IAM users, groups, and roles have no access to AWS resources. IAM policies are the means by which privileges are granted to users, groups, or roles. It is recommended that IAM policies be applied directly to groups and roles but not users. Assigning privileges at the group or role level reduces the complexity of access management as the number of users grow. Reducing access management complexity may in-turn reduce opportunity for a principal to inadvertently receive or retain excessive privileges.

message: AWS CloudShellFullAccess policy is not attached to any IAM entity.
status_detail: AWS CloudShellFullAccess policy is not attached to any IAM entity.

finding_info.desc: This control checks whether an IAM identity (user, role, or group) has the AWS managed policy AWSCloudShellFullAccess attached. The control fails if an IAM identity has the AWSCloudShellFullAccess policy attached.
finding_info.title: Check if IAM identities (users,groups,roles) have the AWSCloudShellFullAccess policy attached.
types: Software and Configuration Checks/AWS Security Best Practices/CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark

remediation.desc: Detach the AWSCloudShellFullAccess policy from the IAM identity to restrict excessive permissions and adhere to the principle of least privilege.

risk_details: Attaching the AWSCloudShellFullAccess policy to IAM identities grants broad permissions, including internet access and file transfer capabilities, which can lead to security risks such as data exfiltration. The principle of least privilege should be followed to avoid excessive permissions.

message: IAM Role CspmMonitorAws does not have AdministratorAccess policy.
status_detail: IAM Role CspmMonitorAws does not have AdministratorAccess policy.

finding_info.desc: Ensure IAM Roles do not have AdministratorAccess policy attached
finding_info.title: Ensure IAM Roles do not have AdministratorAccess policy attached

remediation.desc: Apply the principle of least privilege. Instead of AdministratorAccess, assign only the permissions necessary for specific roles and tasks. Create custom IAM policies with minimal permissions based on the principle of least privilege.

risk_details: The AWS-managed AdministratorAccess policy grants all actions for all AWS services and for all resources in the account and as such exposes the customer to a significant data leakage threat. It should be granted very conservatively. For granting access to 3rd party vendors, consider using alternative managed policies, such as ViewOnlyAccess or SecurityAudit.

message: IAM Role CspmMonitorAws has read-only access but is not cross account.
status_detail: IAM Role CspmMonitorAws has read-only access but is not cross account.

finding_info.desc: Ensure IAM Roles do not have ReadOnlyAccess access for external AWS accounts
finding_info.title: Ensure IAM Roles do not have ReadOnlyAccess access for external AWS accounts

remediation.desc: Remove the AWS-managed ReadOnlyAccess policy from all roles that have a trust policy, including third-party cloud accounts, or remove third-party cloud accounts from the trust policy of all roles that need the ReadOnlyAccess policy.

risk_details: The AWS-managed ReadOnlyAccess policy is highly potent and exposes the customer to a significant data leakage threat. It should be granted very conservatively. For granting access to 3rd party vendors, consider using alternative managed policies, such as ViewOnlyAccess or SecurityAudit.

message: Root account has a virtual MFA instead of a hardware MFA device enabled.
status_detail: Root account has a virtual MFA instead of a hardware MFA device enabled.

finding_info.desc: Ensure only hardware MFA is enabled for the root account
finding_info.title: Ensure only hardware MFA is enabled for the root account

remediation.desc: Using IAM console navigate to Dashboard and expand Activate MFA on your root account. If using AWS Organizations, consider enabling Centralized Root Management and removing individual root credentials.

risk_details: The root account is the most privileged user in an AWS account. MFA adds an extra layer of protection on top of a user name and password. With MFA enabled when a user signs in to an AWS website they will be prompted for their user name and password as well as for an authentication code from their AWS MFA device. For Level 2 it is recommended that the root account be protected with only a hardware MFA.

message: MFA is enabled for root account.
status_detail: MFA is enabled for root account.

finding_info.desc: Ensure MFA is enabled for the root account
finding_info.title: Ensure MFA is enabled for the root account

remediation.desc: Using IAM console navigate to Dashboard and expand Activate MFA on your root account. If using AWS Organizations, consider enabling Centralized Root Management and removing individual root credentials.

risk_details: The root account is the most privileged user in an AWS account. MFA adds an extra layer of protection on top of a user name and password. With MFA enabled when a user signs in to an AWS website they will be prompted for their user name and password as well as for an authentication code from their AWS MFA device. When virtual MFA is used for root accounts it is recommended that the device used is NOT a personal device but rather a dedicated mobile device (tablet or phone) that is managed to be kept charged and secured independent of any individual personal devices. (non-personal virtual MFA) This lessens the risks of losing access to the MFA due to device loss / trade-in or if the individual owning the device is no longer employed at the company.

message: User <root_account> does not have access keys.
status_detail: User <root_account> does not have access keys.

finding_info.desc: Ensure access keys are rotated every 90 days or less
finding_info.title: Ensure access keys are rotated every 90 days or less

remediation.desc: Use the credential report to  ensure  access_key_X_last_rotated  is less than 90 days ago.

risk_details: Access keys consist of an access key ID and secret access key which are used to sign programmatic requests that you make to AWS. AWS users need their own access keys to make programmatic calls to AWS from the AWS Command Line Interface (AWS CLI)- Tools for Windows PowerShell- the AWS SDKs- or direct HTTP calls using the APIs for individual AWS services. It is recommended that all access keys be regularly rotated.

message: User prowler-scan-bot does not have access keys older than 90 days.
status_detail: User prowler-scan-bot does not have access keys older than 90 days.

finding_info.title: Ensure access keys are rotated every 90 days or less

remediation.desc: Use the credential report to  ensure  access_key_X_last_rotated  is less than 90 days ago.

risk_details: Access keys consist of an access key ID and secret access key which are used to sign programmatic requests that you make to AWS. AWS users need their own access keys to make programmatic calls to AWS from the AWS Command Line Interface (AWS CLI)- Tools for Windows PowerShell- the AWS SDKs- or direct HTTP calls using the APIs for individual AWS services. It is recommended that all access keys be regularly rotated.

message: SecurityAudit policy attached to role DefenderForCloud-Ciem.
status_detail: SecurityAudit policy attached to role DefenderForCloud-Ciem.

finding_info.desc: Ensure a Security Audit role has been created to conduct security audits
finding_info.title: Ensure a Security Audit role has been created to conduct security audits

remediation.desc: Create an IAM role for conduct security audits with AWS.

risk_details: Creating an IAM role with a security audit policy provides a clear separation of duties between the security team and other teams within the organization. This helps to ensure that security-related activities are performed by authorized individuals with the appropriate expertise and access permissions.

message: AWS Support Access policy is not attached to any role.
status_detail: AWS Support Access policy is not attached to any role.

finding_info.desc: Ensure a support role has been created to manage incidents with AWS Support
finding_info.title: Ensure a support role has been created to manage incidents with AWS Support

remediation.desc: Create an IAM role for managing incidents with AWS.

risk_details: AWS provides a support center that can be used for incident notification and response, as well as technical support and customer services. Create an IAM Role to allow authorized users to manage incidents with AWS Support.

message: User <root_account> does not have access keys.
status_detail: User <root_account> does not have access keys.

finding_info.desc: Ensure unused User Access Keys are disabled
finding_info.title: Ensure unused User Access Keys are disabled

remediation.desc: Find the credentials that they were using and ensure that they are no longer operational. Ideally, you delete credentials if they are no longer needed. You can always recreate them at a later date if the need arises. At the very least, you should change the password or deactivate the access keys so that the former users no longer have access.

risk_details: To increase the security of your AWS account, remove IAM user credentials (that is, passwords and access keys) that are not needed. For example, when users leave your organization or no longer need AWS access.

message: User prowler-scan-bot does not have unused access keys for 45 days.
status_detail: User prowler-scan-bot does not have unused access keys for 45 days.

finding_info.desc: Ensure unused User Access Keys are disabled
finding_info.title: Ensure unused User Access Keys are disabled

remediation.desc: Find the credentials that they were using and ensure that they are no longer operational. Ideally, you delete credentials if they are no longer needed. You can always recreate them at a later date if the need arises. At the very least, you should change the password or deactivate the access keys so that the former users no longer have access.

risk_details: To increase the security of your AWS account, remove IAM user credentials (that is, passwords and access keys) that are not needed. For example, when users leave your organization or no longer need AWS access.

message: IAM User prowler-scan-bot has AdministratorAccess policy attached.
status_detail: IAM User prowler-scan-bot has AdministratorAccess policy attached.

finding_info.desc: This check ensures that no IAM users in your AWS account have the 'AdministratorAccess' policy attached. IAM users with this policy have unrestricted access to all AWS services and resources, which poses a significant security risk if misused.
finding_info.title: Ensure No IAM Users Have Administrator Access Policy

remediation.desc: Replace the 'AdministratorAccess' policy with more specific permissions that follow the Principle of Least Privilege. Consider implementing IAM roles such as 'IAM Master' and 'IAM Manager' to manage permissions more securely.

risk_details: IAM users with administrator-level permissions can perform any action on any resource in your AWS environment. If these permissions are granted to users unnecessarily or to individuals without sufficient knowledge, it can lead to security vulnerabilities, data leaks, data loss, or unexpected charges.

message: User prowler-scan-bot does not have console access enabled or is unused.
status_detail: User prowler-scan-bot does not have console access enabled or is unused.

finding_info.desc: Ensure unused user console access are disabled
finding_info.title: Ensure unused user console access are disabled

remediation.desc: Find the credentials that they were using and ensure that they are no longer operational. Ideally, you delete credentials if they are no longer needed. You can always recreate them at a later date if the need arises. At the very least, you should change the password or deactivate the access keys so that the former users no longer have access.

risk_details: To increase the security of your AWS account, remove IAM user credentials (that is, passwords and access keys) that are not needed. For example, when users leave your organization or no longer need AWS access.

message: User prowler-scan-bot does not have any type of MFA enabled.
status_detail: User prowler-scan-bot does not have any type of MFA enabled.

finding_info.desc: Check if IAM users have Hardware MFA enabled.
finding_info.title: Check if IAM users have Hardware MFA enabled.

remediation.desc: Enable hardware MFA device for an IAM user from the AWS Management Console, the command line, or the IAM API.

risk_details: Hardware MFA is preferred over virtual MFA.

message: User prowler-scan-bot does not have Console Password enabled.
status_detail: User prowler-scan-bot does not have Console Password enabled.

finding_info.desc: Ensure multi-factor authentication (MFA) is enabled for all IAM users that have a console password.
finding_info.title: Ensure multi-factor authentication (MFA) is enabled for all IAM users that have a console password.

remediation.desc: Enable MFA for the user's account. MFA is a simple best practice that adds an extra layer of protection on top of your user name and password. Recommended to use hardware keys over virtual MFA.

risk_details: Unauthorized access to this critical account if password is not secure or it is disclosed in any way.

message: User <root_account> does not have access keys or uses the access keys configured.
status_detail: User <root_account> does not have access keys or uses the access keys configured.

finding_info.desc: Do not setup access keys during initial user setup for all IAM users that have a console password
finding_info.title: Do not setup access keys during initial user setup for all IAM users that have a console password

remediation.desc: From the IAM console: generate credential report and disable not required keys.

risk_details: AWS console defaults the checkbox for creating access keys to enabled. This results in many access keys being generated unnecessarily. In addition to unnecessary credentials, it also generates unnecessary management work in auditing and rotating these keys. Requiring that additional steps be taken by the user after their profile has been created will give a stronger indication of intent that access keys are (a) necessary for their work and (b) once the access key is established on an account that the keys may be in use somewhere in the organization.

status_detail: User prowler-scan-bot does not have access keys or uses the access keys configured.

finding_info.title: Do not setup access keys during initial user setup for all IAM users that have a console password

remediation.desc: From the IAM console: generate credential report and disable not required keys.

risk_details: AWS console defaults the checkbox for creating access keys to enabled. This results in many access keys being generated unnecessarily. In addition to unnecessary credentials, it also generates unnecessary management work in auditing and rotating these keys. Requiring that additional steps be taken by the user after their profile has been created will give a stronger indication of intent that access keys are (a) necessary for their work and (b) once the access key is established on an account that the keys may be in use somewhere in the organization.

status_detail: User <root_account> does not have 2 active access keys.

finding_info.desc: Check if IAM users have two active access keys
finding_info.title: Check if IAM users have two active access keys

remediation.desc: Avoid using long lived access keys.

risk_details: Access Keys could be lost or stolen. It creates a critical risk.

finding_info.title: Check if IAM users have two active access keys

risk_details: Access Keys could be lost or stolen. It creates a critical risk.

message: User prowler-scan-bot has long lived credentials with access to other services than IAM or STS.
status_detail: User prowler-scan-bot has long lived credentials with access to other services than IAM or STS.

finding_info.desc: Ensure users make use of temporary credentials assuming IAM roles
finding_info.title: Ensure users make use of temporary credentials assuming IAM roles

remediation.desc: As a best practice, use temporary security credentials (IAM roles) instead of creating long-term credentials like access keys, and don't create AWS account root user access keys.

risk_details: As a best practice, use temporary security credentials (IAM roles) instead of creating long-term credentials like access keys, and don't create AWS account root user access keys.
